candidates.stmt:
I first fell in love with Southampton as a student, and since moving back here, I've become aware of just how much potential this city has.

Banister & Polygon is at its heart, but is being let down by substandard, overpriced housing, rubbish lining the streets, and the council's failure to think holistically about travel around the city.

As a proud trans woman, I have a history of fighting and advocating for my siblings in the LGBTQ+ community. I am standing as a council candidate to take that same energy and fight for every member of this ward and city

I am a local businessman and publican. I am free of national party political dogma and will focus on the topics that matter to the local community.  I will force greater transparency on how much money the council generates from local tax payers and how they spend it.  I will also prioritise on matters relating to developing business and personal prosperity.

I am tenacious, a proven successful businessmen and serial winner.  I will fight like hell for what matters.

Politics often feels like a closed-door conversation, but it belongs to us all. I joined the Green Party over a decade ago because it prioritized people, our communities and our planet over big donors and short-term politics.

As a local worker, resident, and school governor, I see the reality of our city every day. I've watched living standards fall, school budgets stagnate, and homelessness increase. I refuse to stay on the sidelines any longer. Our communities, our local businesses, our families, and our green spaces need a new type of champion. Together, we can build a thriving, fairer future.

John Noon has represented Bargate ward for over 20 years and has fought hard to implement the Licencing of Houses of Multiple Occupation, the building of new houses for rent and a reliable bus service serving the ward including Ocean Village when the bus company threatened to stop it. John works tirelessly for residents and is out every week in all weathers knocking on doors to hear what you are concerned about.
If re-elected he will continue to fight for:
An Increase in the number of Police on the streets; the city must get its fair share of the police budget 
 Building more affordable rented accommodation and new ideas for the provision of homes for homeless families such as the HUB Development.
Improvements in Clean Air and Labour’s Green City Charter.

Hi, I'm Sam. I was educated in local schools and at Southampton University and currently work as a healthcare manager.

Both nationally and locally our politics is broken. Residents in Coxford have been let down by the council. Residents finally want to see some local investment to catch up with the rest of our thriving city.

I want to see public transport provide a real alternative to parking on our resident's streets, proper management of weekend traffic to the Sports Centre, and investment bought in to improve shopping facilities in Lordshill.

As someone that has always lived in Southampton, should I be elected I will fight hard on behalf of the residents to deliver real change.

As a former resident here, I'd like to be your future favourite councillor! I'll do my best to bring about positive change, protect our green spaces, and cut down on crime. Our party might have set policies, but my personal policies are YOUR policies.

I'll hold regular surgeries; where's the human in a faceless councillor? You can count on me to be there beyond the election period and in the community. The green menace never disappoints!

I'm autistic and only 18-years-old. I'm taking this election on during my A-levels because I genuinely care. Bassett deserves better, more involved leaders.

Hello, I have lived on the ladder all of my life and have experienced lots of the cuts to our public services first hand under both Tory and Labour austerity. We can beat this by coming together as a community and fighting for the funding and services we need which is something only TUSC stands for, we wont reverse austerity by carrying out more austerity as is either explicitly or implicity promised by every other party.

Every TUSC candidate is mandated to run on these minimum policy platforms:
1. Oppose all cuts and closures to council services, jobs, pay and conditions. Resist the privatisation of services or their transfer to social enterprises or ‘arms-length’ management organisations which are the first steps to their privatisation.

2. Reject council tax, rent and service charge increases for working-class people to make up for cuts in central funding.

3. Vote for councils to use their reserves and prudential borrowing powers to avoid making cuts in their 2026-2027 budgets and demand from the Labour government the additional funding needed to make up any future shortfall.

4. Refuse to co-operate with any commissioners or ‘envoys’ appointed by the government to attempt to impose cuts on local services.

5. Support only democratically debated local Climate Emergency plans that create new employment, build flood defences, reduce emissions and improve air quality and the local environment, while protecting the jobs, pay and living standards of all workers.

6. Fight for united working-class struggle against racism, sexism and all forms of oppression, including against refugees, asylum seekers and migrants.  Back all workers’ struggles against government policies making ordinary people pay for the crisis.

I will of course work with local residents and allies to build this out.

I moved to Southampton three years ago after retiring as a housing officer and a youth and community worker with over thirty years experience. I am currently a governor on the Mental Health Trust and a member of my local Neighbourhood Watch. I have a reputation of speaking up for the people that I represented when I was the Mayor of Boston and I will do the same for the people of Sholing. If elected, I will fight for better services like buses, sorting out footpaths and potholes, promoting 20MPH limits and tackling speeding. I would like to see more youth provision for young people and more visible policing in our communities. I am very concerned about the effect of the cost of living crisis on the people of Sholing.

Hello, I’m Amy and I’m the Green Party candidate for Bevois. I’m passionate about helping residents and students across the ward, to help everyone get the most out of our wonderful city.

When I joined the University I founded the Young Greens Society there, and since I have regularly attended meetings with students, residents and political activists to ensure that we have a strong student voice in the city, but at the same time not one that leaves residents behind, so that we can create the best city for all of us.

I have lived in Southampton for almost 40 years, 24 in Bitterne Park.  If elected I would promote active and sustainable transport to benefit the health of local residents and improve air quality, including better provision for cyclists and more 20 mph roads.  I would work to protect and improve the parks and green spaces in Bitterne Park and support measures to tackle pollution of the River Itchen.  I would like to see support for small businesses to ensure that the Triangle continues to thrive for voluntary groups such as Friends of Riverside Park and Friends of Cobbett Road Library.

Its time for reforms. The uni party has failed us all and we need a fresh, new and invigorating Party in the form of Reform UK which puts families, communities and country first. We need to get back to basics, cut waste and focus on the peoples needs.

On May 7th 2026, I will be seeking your help once more to become Councillor for our area covering **Aldermoor, Coxford, Lordshill and Lordswood.**

In 2024 at the last attempt, it was my closest showing yet. My vote share was raised and I **was just 46 votes behind Labour, and over 750 votes ahead of all other parties.**

Over the last two years two of your councillors changed party. One from Labour to the Greens, who came fifth in 2024, and one from Labour to the Lib Dems, who came fourth in 2024. In that time you know I was still fighting for you.

As few examples of Action for Our Ward

- **Lordshill Housing Office** 
I have had success in removing the eyesore of the Lordshill Housing office. I demonstrated the yearly costs it was accruing while just a shell and had been left too long to be turned into a usable site again.

- **Coxford / Tremona Road Junction:** 
Rather than simply acknowledging issues, I found that the University and Hospital were redeveloping the site on the corner. Through my Conservative colleagues we got this raised at full council level to ask if the Council were seeking the ability to renew this junction that has the City’s most bus routes outside the City Centre

- **Exposing Wasteful Spending:** 
A further freedom of information request exposed the costs of the new City “welcome” signs that do nothing to demonstrate the history or gravitas of our standing as cruise capital.

- **Fighting to Restore Street Lighting:**
I’ve fought the switch-off since day one. By identifying incorrectly unlit pathways, I successfully got the lights turned back on in key areas. Now, I’m fighting to restore full lighting so our 24-hour workers can feel safe. You can still sign the petition: **www.change.org/24hlights4soton**

And finally of course, when any resident in need, I will seek to help and find a satisfactory resolution.

Give me the chance to be your councillor and you give me a platform to do more for every person in this ward, to push further for

I am a local resident who has lived in Southampton for 28 years. If elected my goal is to help the people of Coxford with issues you bring to me and represent those views to Southampton City Council.
As a Green, I aim to help make Southampton a Greener and more pleasant place to live.
As an engineer I have worked in industry and also run my own part time business, so will understand the issues around being self-employed. 
Being Green is about hope not hate as practiced by other parties. So let's move first and Vote Green.

''The system is set-up to line the pockets of the super-rich and to hell with the rest of us. We stand for what our communities need, starting with a fully funded NHS, kick out the private profiteers and full pay restoration for all staff.''

I’m committed to providing a strong, reasoned voice of opposition in the chamber, helping to shape council policy in favour of the people in our city. That means relentlessly fighting for council housing in the city, getting a grip on the special education services, and working tirelessly on the issues the residents of Freemantle care most about.
I have already worked on holding the council’s housing scrutiny panel to account, additionally I’m working on issues regarding bin provisions for other residents I have spoken with. I would be thrilled to have the opportunity to continue this vital work formally.

I have lived in Southampton for over 20 years, during which time I have worked in local music education, visiting local schools.

I cycle between all of my schools, and I’m keen to encourage active travel as a means to improving public health and reducing the city's pollution and congestion, I see how it impacts the youngsters I teach in schools.  I  want to use my voice on planning committees to ensure that building affordable homes takes priority over other forms of development.

I want to be a third Green councillor, helping the party to make a real difference.

I have lived in Millbrook for many years. I believe in keeping all our green spaces, parks and play areas. They also need to be maintained and improved.

The environment, social justice and council housing are key priorities.

As a former Trade Union Senior Representative for many years I know how important well paid jobs are for the local community as well as thriving local small businesses.

The sewage entering our rivers is a disgrace.

Bringing the Water Industry and our other Utilities back into Public Ownership will help with the cost of living crisis as will a wealth tax.

I'm Soham Bandyopadhyay, a doctor in Southampton. Like a lot of people here, I'm tired of watching the basics get harder while the bills keep going up.

In my day job I see what happens when systems stop working: the delays that turn small problems into crises; the admin and duplication that swallow time and money; the knock-on effects on families, carers, and people just trying to get by. Around the city, you feel the same pattern: the cost of living squeeze, pressures on social care, stretched local services, and small businesses trying to survive while bureaucracy grows.

My view is simple: council politics should be about the essentials, done well, done transparently, and done with respect for taxpayers. That means cutting waste and refocusing spending where it actually improves daily life: social care that supports dignity and independence, essential services residents rely on, and practical backing for local businesses that keep the city moving and people in work.

I'm not a career politician. I'm used to working under pressure, making decisions with imperfect information, and being accountable for outcomes: not headlines. I want a council that does the same: clear priorities, transparent spending, and delivery you can see on your street and in your services.

That's why I'm standing to become a Reform councillor. The current approach isn't working, and tinkering at the edges won't fix it. Southampton needs reform that’s practical, disciplined, and focused on the basics. I’d be proud to help deliver it.

I'm a Southampton resident standing in Peartree for the Green Party in the May local elections. As expected I am environmentally conscious, but also very conscious of social justice and the cost of living.

I work in mental health and volunteer in drug addiction recovery, and would love to direct my political energies into representing the people of this great city.

The present is very uncertain and the future even less so. I am under no illusions about the scale of the problems we face and will do all I can to fight for the best possible outcomes for everybody.

Southampton-born Rob Naish lives in Itchen and works for a defence contractor based in Portsmouth.

He is particular concerned to maintain and improve Southampton's green environment, and has been active in conservation in Itchen.

His special interest is in leisure services and sport.

Rob is Treasurer of Southampton Liberal Democrats.

An active member of the community, I’m committed to Portswood, to ensure we get the clean air, well-funded care, quality housing, and healthy rivers we all deserve.

Having raised deputations at Southampton City Council questioning cuts of £39m of funds from Children and Adult Social care, and highlighting that the city is not acting on idling and air quality outside schools, I will continue holding the administration to account.

Bills continue to rise, wages stagnate,  austerity bites, with ever-increasing cuts and poor value for residents. I stand for real, practical change and hope - a break from business as usual.

“Local, Approachable, Hard -Working

It has been an honour to represent you for the last three years and I have enjoyed meeting so many of you through casework. Together we have tackled  topics such as waste, drainage, renters rights, school or adult social care concerns and combatting fly tipping. 
I have had some successes, such as getting drug litter cleared, getting Friends of Janaway Park started, getting three new public bins in and two unused ,vandalised phone boxes removed. Not everything has worked out as we wanted. I opposed the student development on the Broadway for example. I also have  campaigns that are ongoing, like pushing for a replacement for the Wheeler bus on Highfield lane and Westwood Rd.

Your labour council will be investing in a new enforcement team. This will help improve the district centre and other areas that suffer from fly tipping or other violations. We are also investing  in our green spaces, so important for our wellbeing.

I live in St Denys and am active in both community and litter picking groups there.  I am a keen walker with the family dog and I have been a Saints season ticket holder for over 20 years. “

Reform UK have great policies that would immediately have a positive effect on everyone's income. Raising the tax threshold to £20,000 would immediately help those in low-paid employment.  Reducing VAT on energy bills and reducing fuel duty would lower inflation, thereby benefiting both consumers and businesses.

Lowering corporation tax from the record high 25% would attract foreign investment and manufacturing to the UK.  Future employment prospects would be more certain, unlike now.  These policies will stimulate growth and make the country more prosperous.

The UK needs a workable immigration system such as other modern countries operate to enable us to choose those that would make a contribution to our nation. Having valid health insurance and employment skills to enter the UK would be just two requirements.

I am fully behind our party's stance over illegal migration and the plan to stop the boats and take the occupants of those boats, back to France.  The Conservatives wont stop the boats. Labour have agreed with Brussels, to fly migrants into the UK from the EU.

I support reducing foreign aid and redirecting that money to help rebuild public services that have been decimated through underfunding.

Reforming the NHS means redistributing NHS finances to enable the training and retaining of doctors. Doing away with those wasteful management posts listed as diversity and equality officers, would leave more cash for patient care. Reform sees everyone as one people in one nation, regardless of ethnicity, colour or religion. Abolishing degree entry for nurses would attract more people into the profession.

Our country needs an affordable integrated transport system if we are to reduce congestion on our roads and combat subsequent pollution.  Petrol and diesel vehicles should be retained until viable alternatives are ready.

Consecutive Tory governments have reduced our armed forces to a dangerous level, unable to respond to overseas threats.  Reform UK is committe

Enough is enough!
One seat needs to change for a positive future. 
My pledges to Redbridge: 
1. Protect parks and green spaces, 
2. Better roads and pavements, 
3. Improved Housing not in-fill, 
4. No to Council cuts, 
5. No more wasting YOUR money, 
6. Listening to YOUR priorities.

Residents of Millbrook, Maybush and Redbridge say: "Andrew Pope stands up for our area. Vote Andrew Pope Independent Network on the 7th May because he isn't told what to do by political parties. Andrew is Putting You First. Only Andrew Pope can beat Labour here. Enough is enough. Help him to help you and yours."

I have lived in Millbrook since 1997 with my husband and now 18 year old son. We are very lucky to live in such a green area of the city.

My priorities are affordable social housing, not selling off our council homes. Ensuring our water is clean and not threatened by sewage, agricultural runoff or chemical pollution. The water companies need to be brought back into public ownership.

I want a fully public, properly funded health and social care system, which keeps the profit motive of private companies well away from our NHS.

Simon has been the Liberal Democrat candidate for Redbridge since 2010, and has been working hard for the area. The work he has been doing includes:

- Having dangerous potholes fixed, and some roads resurfaced

- Supporting the 20mph zone

- Campaigning for a Park & Ride scheme for the city

Simon campaigned for the UK to remain in the EU, and continues to campaign against the damage being caused to our economy, our citizens, and our standing in the world as a result of the Convervatives' incompetent handling of the Brexit crisis. He also volunteers locally for a national parenting charity.

Some of the issues that are of particular interest are the environment, sustainable energy, education, civil liberties and public transport.

I was born and raised in Southampton and have lived in the city my entire life, being brought up in Shirley and living there throughout my childhood until my early 20s. 

I’ve always been active in the local community and growing up in Shirley, I learned the value of community as my mum and I often relied on community initiatives – these were important then and they are even more so important now. 

I have campaigned across Southampton for Labour in every general and local election for the last 7 years and speaking with residents on the doorstep is something I really love. Every week, I campaign in Shirley with fellow Labour members and, combined with it being a place I know very well anyway, it’s given me a great oversight and further understanding of the issues residents face.  Spending my entire working life working within local government has given me a really clear idea of what issues council’s face and, as a resident of the city, I also understand the importance of having a strong and principled Council, being held to account and backing its residents to make the city a better place. 

The Labour Party and the City of Southampton, specifically Shirley, are two of the things I am most passionate about and I will do absolutely everything to ensure that I am best representing myself, the Party and most importantly, the residents.

I have been asked to stand again for the Green Party after a large increase in the Green vote at the recent by-election.

I have lived in Shirley for 30 years, and am a committed and active member of the local community through involvement with a local church, as a treasurer for a ‘Friends of ‘group and supporting research at the hospital.

I am passionate about improving residents health and wellbeing, housing, employment prospects, the local environment plus strengthening local communities.

I love the amount of green space in Southampton. The Sholing Valley Study Centre is definitely one of the best, I was delighted and honoured to become a trustee.

I am asking for your vote on to ensure that Southampton remains on top on protecting and enjoying nature. There are many concerning issues, not least the availability of decent housing stock and well kept roads, the regular collection of household waste and employment opportunities in attracting forward thinking and progressive employers.

We in the Green Party want you to love where you live and your priorities will always be ours.

I am a local resident who has lived in Swaythling for over 25 years and am a committed and active member of the local community. I run an environmental group in the city and helped set up a repair cafe in the ward.

I worked at Southampton General for 30 years and care deeply about the state of our local NHS services. I am keen to expand the Green Party's influence on the council.

Since coming to Southampton for university four years ago it's been fantastic to live in and around Swaythling and to get to live in such a wonderful community.

It's been wonderful to see what Southampton Labour have already delivered in cleaning up the city, and if elected I promise to campaign for stronger action on fly tipping and more investment in waste collection.

I also know how important it is for young people to have something meaningful to do and I promise to work to improve the affordability and quality of our public leisure facilities as well as investing in youth clubs to provide residents with more opportunities.

"Andy Frampton was born in the ward and lives locally with his family. His experience as a Regional Secretary with UNITE means that he knows the problems that affect working families and his local campaigning has put him in touch with the problems facing Bitterne, Thornhill and parts of Sholing.

Andy says: ‘My personal pledge to you is that if elected as your councillor I will work for a new kind of local government politics: more inclusive, walking the streets of the area, listening and acting on your concerns whether it be: > Anti-Social Behaviour > Pot Holes > Building Repairs > Planning Applications > Parking Problems

I will be there. Making our area a “better place to live” in which no one is left behind."

I'm a local resident standing in Thornhill, the place I've called home for nearly 50 years. I love Thornhill and want it to thrive.

I've been part of this community all my life. As a teenager, I campaigned for a skate park and football pitch on Hinkler Green.

Thornhill is full of good people who look out for each other, with a strong sense of community. It's special.
I want better roads, safer streets, more activities for our children, warmer homes with lower bills, and protected green spaces.

I'll listen, bring people together, and give Thornhill a strong Green voice.

I am standing in the local elections for Woolston because I care about this community and I want to make a real difference.

I have spent my working career supporting people who need it most. I started as a Support Worker, working directly with vulnerable young people, and over the years I have worked my way up into management and now business development. I have seen the system from every level. Even now, despite moving into business development, I still pick up shifts because I genuinely enjoy helping people and staying connected to the frontline.

I have sat with young people in crisis, sometimes when they feel like they have no one else. I have seen how quickly things can go wrong when support is not there, and how powerful it is when someone simply listens and steps in to help. Those moments stay with you. They change how you see things.

I am not a career politician. I am someone who has had to deal with real situations, make difficult decisions, and take responsibility when it matters most.

I am standing because I want Woolston to have someone who genuinely cares and will stand up for it. Someone who listens, who shows up, and who does not walk away when things get difficult.

My priorities are simple and focused on what residents actually deal with every day. I want to improve council housing by pushing for a rapid repair plan and making sure standards are properly enforced. Too many people are waiting too long for basic issues to be fixed, and that is not acceptable.

I will push for better roads, pothole repairs and overall maintenance, including reviewing existing contracts to make sure work is properly completed and signed off. Residents should not be paying for work that is not done properly.

I want to improve bin collections, street cleaning and waste services so our area is kept clean and looked after. At the same time, we all have a role to play in taking pride in where we live.

Housing is also a major concern. I will work to address the impact of

I’ve lived in Southampton since 1984, I recently retired from SCC Adult Social Care, I managed a Day Centre in Woolston. I have been an active volunteer with a national children's agency and attend a local community hub in Weston.

My ambition is for the water of Weston Shore to be clean enough to swim in, this needs to be achieved through a multi-agency approach.

Let's work together for the services we deserve and a city we can truly be proud of.
Vote for me in the local elections so that I can take your concerns directly to the Council.
